task_completion.description: Verify that the user has joined the 'NoisyCafe' meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'localhost:8080/NoisyCafe' or the room name 'NoisyCafe' is clearly visible in the Jitsi Meet interface across the screenshots.

task_completion.description: Verify that the settings menu is captured in the settings screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot ptt_settings.png shows the Jitsi Meet settings dialog open.

task_completion.description: Verify that the microphone is inactive in the inactive screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot ptt_inactive.png shows the Jitsi Meet interface with the microphone icon crossed out, indicating a muted/inactive state.

task_completion.description: Verify that the microphone is active in the active screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot ptt_active.png shows the Jitsi Meet interface with the microphone active (unmuted), and potentially a visual indicator that push-to-talk is in use (such as a 'Push to talk' label or highlighted microphone).

integrity.description: Verify that the screenshots show a genuine web browser running Jitsi Meet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should display a standard web browser interface (e.g., Chromium or Firefox) rendering the Jitsi Meet web application, not a text editor or image viewer.

integrity.description: Verify that the inactive and active screenshots show distinct microphone states.
integrity.visual_evidence: ptt_inactive.png and ptt_active.png must not be identical; they should clearly show the microphone transitioning from a muted state to an unmuted/active state.

integrity.description: Verify that the screenshots are taken within the agent's desktop environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should capture the actual desktop or browser window, showing genuine GUI interaction rather than pre-downloaded or fabricated images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Jitsi Meet is hosted at http://localhost:8080. The target room is 'NoisyCafe'. Push-to-Talk is a built-in feature in Jitsi Meet activated by holding the SPACE key. Note that while the task asks to enable it in settings, Push-to-Talk is always available by default; the settings screenshot should at least show the Jitsi Meet settings menu. The microphone should be muted in the inactive screenshot and unmuted/active in the active screenshot.
